pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: *"The Backpass Assassin" wouldn't know a forward pass if it hit him in the face, and "Mustard Boy" is equally clueless — this is a contest of incompetence.* Gayden's England, always the bridesmaid and never the bride, is set to face Zach's Ghana in what promises to be a laughable display of misguided drafting. England's inconsistency should've scared off anyone who knows their soccer, but here we are, stuck with The Backpass Assassin, still fumbling around with more dropped points than I've got career goals. Meanwhile, Mustard Boy likely picked Ghana because he recognized their name from his FIFA video game sessions — seriously, Zach, have you ever watched a full match or just familiarized yourself with the pause button? This clash of futbol dunces will be a trainwreck, but let me be clear: England will squeak out a win because Zach's management is as aimless as a blindfolded penalty taker.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner's France Dominates But Senegal Falls Short Against Norway
pundit_takes.subtitle: Proving once again that points are like perfect attendance awards.

pundit_takes.headline: Backpass Assassin's England Poised To Outmaneuver Mustard Boy's Ghana
pundit_takes.subtitle: Expecting more tactical retreats than a game of chess.

pundit_takes.headline: Ghost Pepper's Quiet Consistency Leaves Him One Point Off The Top
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin shows that slow and steady almost wins the race.

pundit_takes.headline: Noisemaker Rafe In Top Ranking With Ecuador And Mexico
pundit_takes.subtitle: Who knew drafting teams that actually qualified could pay off?

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner's holding firm at the top, but Rafe’s chaos is just 3 points behind.
Rafe's Tier-1 Germany is his saving grace; his draft picks were a dumpster fire.
Rafe vs. Devin: teaching the old guard a lesson with a +6 point differential.
Sim got Devin's surging predictions right, but who saw Rafe's havoc coming?
